nodes.label: AI as collaborative debugger and code improver
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable source requiring full verification
nodes.grounding_span: I'll ask it the question and give what I already have and then ask how to fix an issue

nodes.label: AI as last resort after self and colleague effort
nodes.label_negative: AI as first resort without prior effort
nodes.grounding_span: If I am stuck for a while and don't know how to handle programming it myself and my colleagues also do not have good suggestions then I will ask AI

nodes.label: AI as tool for error-prone code cleanup
nodes.label_negative: AI introducing new errors in code
nodes.grounding_span: Cleaning up the code for handling line pays and pays in general by replacing all the series of if then else statements with a cleaner method

nodes.label: AI as enhancer of mathematical expertise
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for mathematical skills
nodes.grounding_span: I think that it will always be a tool a trained mathematician uses

nodes.label: AI as future creative partner in game design
nodes.label_negative: AI limited to simple coding tasks only
nodes.grounding_span: potential in some of the creative aspects with coming up with ideas for bonus games or other game features

nodes.label: Code correctness and maintainability
nodes.grounding_span: making it much more difficult to make mistakes when using the code for future games

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I do think its important to maintain those programming skills so I can aid the AI in those steps

nodes.label: Innovation in game design
nodes.grounding_span: coming up with ideas for bonus games or other game features based on parameters it gets fed

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI-assisted code improvement
nodes.grounding_span: It was a combination of the cleaner code and the process

nodes.label: Cautious reliance on AI as last resort
nodes.grounding_span: If I am stuck for a while and don't know how to handle programming it myself and my colleagues also do not have good suggestions then I will ask AI

nodes.label: Optimism about AI's creative potential
nodes.grounding_span: potential in some of the creative aspects with coming up with ideas for bonus games

nodes.label: Hierarchical problem-solving: self first, then colleagues, then AI
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to prefer to start programming it myself and then if I can program it myself I will ask an AI if there are any suggestions it has for improvements

nodes.label: Iterative verification and debugging with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I will then go through its code to make sure it works and doesn't have any bugs and if I do find bugs and I cannot easily fix them on my own I'll ask the AI to fix them
